user.title: Initial Setup
user.description:
## Welcome to Claw Assistant

The **AI orchestration hub and decision-making brain** for Home Assistant.

**Plugin Architecture** — Every conversation agent you install (OpenAI, Google AI, Ollama, local models, etc.) plugs in as a capability module, with Claw Assistant handling unified orchestration, intelligent failover, and deep capability enhancement.

**Natural Language Driven** — Turns the previously impossible into reality, endowing AI with autonomous evolution and persistent memory.

**Zero Barrier to Use** — Supports any creative capability you envision. Simply speak to AI in the most straightforward language, it is fully capable of grasping your intent.

**Synergy as Evolution** — Each capability alone is merely a tool. In concert, AI becomes an around-the-clock intelligent home operator: auditing logs, tracing history, repairing automations, reorganizing dashboards, distilling lessons learned, making every decision sharper than the last.

---

Please **restart Home Assistant** after first install. Click **Submit** to begin setup.

agent_settings.title: Configure AI Agents
agent_settings.description:
Select the conversation agents to use. The system tries each in order until a valid reply is obtained.

▸ **Primary AI** — The default agent that responds first
▸ **Backup AI** — Takes over automatically when the primary fails
▸ **Summary AI** — Optional. Both AIs answer separately, then this one summarizes

init.title: Configuration Center
init.description: Select a feature area to manage

menu_options.agent_settings: Configure AI Agents | Primary, fallback, summarizer chain
menu_options.conversation_settings: Tune Conversation Style | Display, streaming & dimensions
menu_options.user_mapping: IM User Mapping | Link external identities to HA users
menu_options.workspace_editor: Edit Workspace Docs | Master prompt & skill library
menu_options.skill_editor: Manage Installed Skills | Live view & edit

agent_settings.title: Agent Settings
agent_settings.description:
Configure the AI conversation routing chain. The system tries each agent in order until a valid reply is obtained.

▸ **Primary AI** — The default agent used first
▸ **Backup AI** — Automatically used when the primary AI fails
▸ **Summary AI** — Optional. Both AIs answer separately, then this AI summarizes

conversation_settings.title: Conversation Settings
conversation_settings.description: Adjust AI conversation strategy, chat experience, and execution control.

menu_options.conv_dialog: Conversation Strategy | How Replies Are Generated
menu_options.conv_display: Chat Experience | How Chat Is Used

conv_dialog.title: Conversation Strategy
conv_dialog.description: Configure how AI decides and generates replies.

data_description.conversation_mode:
• **Simple** — Show only the final reply, no AI attribution
• **Show source** — Recommended. Prefixes each reply with the responding AI's name, e.g. (deepseek) Reply:
• **Multi-model** — All configured AIs answer the same question simultaneously, each labeled and separated
data_description.enable_web_search: When enabled, the AI can automatically supplement replies with online search results

conv_display.title: Chat Experience
conv_display.description: Configure chat interaction and display behavior.

data_description.enable_sidebar_dock: When enabled, AI conversations are displayed as a fixed right sidebar alongside the main interface; when disabled, the default floating dialog is used.
data_description.enable_tool_details: When enabled, shows full tool call cards (parameters, results, timing) in AI replies. When disabled, only concise progress text is shown.
data_description.enable_tool_progress: When enabled, shows live tool execution progress in the chat (e.g. querying devices, searching). When disabled, tools run silently — ideal for voice pipelines.
data_description.continuous_conversation: When enabled, reopening or activating the frontend chat keeps using the same conversation until /new is sent. Disabled keeps the current per-window behavior.
data_description.enable_sound_notifications: When enabled, plays notification sounds on key events like reply completion and errors. When disabled, all sounds are muted.
data_description.enable_context_status_bar: When enabled, shows context tokens, window age, and current reply elapsed time above the chat input.
data_description.enable_file_upload: When enabled, shows an attach button in the chat input for uploading images, videos, and documents via drag, paste, or click.
data_description.enable_rich_markdown: When enabled, Markdown formatting (bold, tables, code blocks, etc.) is preserved and rendered as rich text; when disabled, all formatting is stripped back to plain text.
data_description.enable_activity_tracking: When enabled, AI can sense your UI actions (page navigation, card clicks, toggle switches, etc.) to better understand what you're working on. When disabled, no actions are collected.

chat_window.name: Chat Window
chat_window.description: Configure chat window presentation and session behavior.

data_description.enable_sidebar_dock: When enabled, AI conversations are displayed as a fixed right sidebar alongside the main interface; when disabled, the default floating dialog is used.
data_description.continuous_conversation: When enabled, reopening or activating the frontend chat keeps using the same conversation until /new is sent. Disabled keeps the current per-window behavior.
data_description.enable_sound_notifications: When enabled, plays notification sounds on key events like reply completion and errors. When disabled, all sounds are muted.

message_display.name: Input & Display
message_display.description: Configure chat input and message display capabilities.

data_description.enable_file_upload: When enabled, shows an attach button in the chat input for uploading images, videos, and documents via drag, paste, or click.
data_description.enable_rich_markdown: When enabled, Markdown formatting (bold, tables, code blocks, etc.) is preserved and rendered as rich text; when disabled, all formatting is stripped back to plain text.
data_description.enable_activity_tracking: When enabled, AI can sense your UI actions (page navigation, card clicks, toggle switches, etc.) to better understand what you're working on. When disabled, no actions are collected.

diagnostics.name: Advanced Diagnostics
diagnostics.description: Configure display of runtime, context, and diagnostic state.

data_description.enable_tool_details: When enabled, shows full tool call cards (parameters, results, timing) in AI replies. When disabled, only concise progress text is shown.
data_description.enable_tool_progress: When enabled, shows live tool execution progress in the chat (e.g. querying devices, searching). When disabled, tools run silently — ideal for voice pipelines.
data_description.enable_context_status_bar: When enabled, shows context tokens, window age, and current reply elapsed time above the chat input.

conv_runtime.title: Execution Control
conv_runtime.description: Configure control rules for AI task execution.

data_description.max_tool_repeat: Max times AI can call the same tool repeatedly. Once exceeded, AI is forced to stop the current approach and try alternatives or admit it cannot solve the problem
data_description.pipeline_timeout: How long the chat window waits for AI to finish. Increase for long tasks or multi-AI chats so AI is not cut off mid-work.

workspace_editor.title: Workspace Document Editor
workspace_editor.description:
**Instructions:**
These documents define the AI assistant's **core personality and behavior** in Markdown format.
Select a document to view or edit. Changes are **saved immediately** without restart.

ws_agents.title: Edit AGENTS.md
ws_agents.description:
**File roles & operating rules**
Defines responsibilities for each workspace document and AI behavioral constraints.
Like a project README — tells the AI what each file does and what actions are allowed.

Markdown format. Saved immediately.

ws_bootstrap.title: Edit BOOTSTRAP.md
ws_bootstrap.description:
**First-run bootstrap**
Only active on first launch. Guides the AI through initial setup (collecting user name, preferences, etc.).
Automatically deactivates once IDENTITY and USER are complete.

Markdown format. Saved immediately.

data_description.bootstrap_active: When enabled, the AI will re-run the bootstrap flow on the next conversation; when disabled, bootstrap is skipped

ws_heartbeat.title: Edit HEARTBEAT.md
ws_heartbeat.description:
**Scheduled follow-ups**
Defines heartbeat behavior rules. The AI periodically executes follow-up tasks.
Examples: check device status, send reminders, trigger automations.

Markdown format. Saved immediately.

ws_identity.title: Edit IDENTITY.md
ws_identity.description:
**Assistant identity**
Defines the AI's name, creature type, personality tags, and representative emoji.
Affects how the AI perceives itself and its reply style.

Example:
`- **Name:** My Assistant`
`- **Creature:** AI Butler`

Markdown format. Saved immediately.

ws_memory.title: Edit MEMORY.md
ws_memory.description:
**User preferences**
Stores long-term user preferences and habits, one per line.
The AI auto-matches relevant entries based on conversation context.

Example:
`- User prefers brief replies`
`- preferred_address: Boss`

Markdown format. Saved immediately.

ws_soul.title: Edit SOUL.md
ws_soul.description:
**Voice & style**
Defines the AI's tone and personality.
Examples: calm and direct, humorous, professionally formal.

Example:
`- Be calm, direct, practical, and concise.`
`- Sound like a reliable operator, not a generic chatbot.`

Markdown format. Saved immediately.

ws_tools.title: Edit TOOLS.md
ws_tools.description:
**Environment notes**
Device info, service names, etc. AI references these during actions.

**Access token (optional):** If AI can't read logs/run commands, add a long-lived token for REST fallback.
Create at: Profile → Long-Lived Access Tokens. Format: `ha_token: eyJ...`

Saved immediately.

ws_user.title: Edit USER.md
ws_user.description:
**User info**
Stores basic user details. The AI uses these to personalize replies.

Example:
`- **Name:** John`
`- **What to call them:** Boss`
`- **Timezone:** America/New_York`

Markdown format. Saved immediately.

skill_editor.title: Installed Skills
skill_editor.description:
**{skill_count} installed skill(s) detected.**
Pick one to view/edit its full Markdown on the next page. The list is read live from `.storage/claw_assistant/skills/`, so newly installed or removed skills appear immediately.

skill_edit.title: Edit skill: {skill_name}

plugin_manager.title: Installed Plugins
plugin_manager.description:
Thanks to the Hermes Agent project, this feature specially supports **Hermes-compatible extension modules**. You can find any compatible plugins on GitHub or other platforms to provide AI with additional tool capabilities (e.g., code analysis, document retrieval, external API calls). Plugins support **hot loading**—enable/disable without restarting Home Assistant. Some plugins may lack descriptions or may not work; use **AI assistance** to explore and modify them, unlocking more capabilities.

---
**{plugin_count} installed plugins.**
Select a plugin to view details, or enable/disable/delete in the next step.
List reads from `.storage/claw_assistant/plugins/` in real-time.

plugin_delete_confirm.title: Delete Plugin: {plugin_name}
plugin_delete_confirm.description: Are you sure you want to delete this plugin? This action cannot be undone.

user_mapping.title: IM User Mapping
user_mapping.description:
**Current mappings:**
{current_mappings}

**Instructions:**
Add: Select IM platform → pick or type external user ID → select HA user.
Remove: Select mapping to delete → submit.

▸ **External User ID** — Dropdown auto-fills known external identities (detected from conversation history). Choose "manual input" at the bottom to type a custom ID.
▸ **Once mapped**, messages from this IM user will automatically link to the selected HA user, with independent persona and memory.

error.invalid_agent: Please select a valid dialogue proxy entity
error.no_agents_available: No valid conversation proxy was found, make sure at least one conversation proxy is available
error.no_installed_skills: No installed skills yet. Install one via the InstallSkill AI tool.
error.no_installed_plugins: No installed plugins yet. Copy Hermes-compatible plugins to the plugins directory.
